mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, it signals severe labor market slack and deteriorating consumer spending power, which directly suppresses corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand recovery, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher near-term corporate earnings surprises and positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 225.0, it indicates accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real discount rates, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 225.0 is followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates directly expand the denominator for valuation multiples and increase aggregate corporate profits, producing a persistent positive correlation with monthly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows a negative or zero Rank IC with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing downturn that precedes earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative price adjustments in the S&P 500 over the following week.
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of falling INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances.
